title: lomatium marginatum
intro: Lomatium marginatum, also known as fern-leaved biscuitroot, is a perennial plant native to the western United States and Canada, belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial, antiviral, and antifungal properties, which help to combat infections and reduce inflammation. The plant's roots and leaves have been used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, fever, and skin problems, and are said to have immunomodulatory effects, enhancing the body's natural defenses. Lomatium marginatum can be prepared into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used in traditional and herbal medicine to promote overall well-being and health.
